run_metadata.notes: Codra handles AI provider API credentials and asset bundles. High-value targets: user stored API keys, proprietary project blueprints, and asset manifests. Vectors: Exposed/unauthenticated serverless functions, bypassing RLS using service keys, and IDORs. (Legacy run: branch and commit metadata were not captured at the time of this audit; file migrated to schema v1.1.0 by PLP-74.)

agent.name: security-and-privacy-auditor
agent.role: Identify practical security and privacy risks with realistic attack scenarios.

findings.title: Critical Authentication Bypass in asset_manifest_save.ts
findings.description: The netlify function `asset_manifest_save.ts` uses `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` to bypass Row Level Security. It trusts the `userId` and `workspaceId` extracted directly from the request payload JSON without validating any JWT or Authorization header. An attacker can forge save requests for any user or workspace, overwriting their assets.

proof_hooks.summary: userId and workspaceId taken from request body without JWT validation
Forge a save request for another user's workspace
steps: Observe the server overwrites the victim's asset bundle using its admin privileges without verifying caller identity

findings.impact: An attacker can overwrite any user's asset bundle by forging the userId and workspaceId in the request body, leading to data loss and unauthorized modification of proprietary project assets.

suggested_fix.approach: Use `await supabase.auth.getUser(token)` to verify the user's identity from the `Authorization` header instead of trusting the request body payload. Alternatively, rely on RLS and use the user's initialized Supabase client rather than the service role key.

findings.title: Unauthenticated Data Exposure in asset_manifest_get.ts
findings.description: The netlify function `asset_manifest_get.ts` fetches asset bundles and assets using the `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (RLS bypassed) by simply taking a `bundleId` from the query string. There is no session validation.

proof_hooks.summary: bundleId taken from query string without any auth check

steps: Observe the server returns full manifest contents without verifying caller identity

findings.impact: Any unauthenticated caller who knows or can guess a bundleId can read the full contents of any user's asset manifest, leaking proprietary project blueprints.

suggested_fix.approach: Implement JWT validation via `supabase.auth.getUser(token)` and verify the authenticated user has access to the workspace that owns the bundle, or use the regular Supabase client to rely on RLS.

next_actions.action: Add JWT validation to asset_manifest_save.ts: use supabase.auth.getUser(token) from Authorization header instead of trusting request body userId/workspaceId
next_actions.rationale: Endpoint trusts caller-supplied identity without verifying the Authorization header, enabling auth bypass.

next_actions.action: Add JWT validation to asset_manifest_get.ts: verify the authenticated user owns the workspace associated with the requested bundleId
next_actions.rationale: Endpoint exposes any bundle to unauthenticated callers who know or guess a bundleId.
